channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Spotify is adding new markets to its music video test
item.description: Spotify is expanding its beta test of adding music videos to the platform by several dozen regions, though it still is unavailable in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: UL Solutions Enters GenAI Evaluation Market with Customized Approach
item.description: UL Solutions joins crowded market with unique evaluation service
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NCSC seeks to expand counterintelligence outreach
item.description: A new national strategy warns that counterintelligence pros have to work more closely with other agencies and the private sector to address threats. The post NCSC seeks to expand counterintelligence outreach first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: IT部門、データセンターの持続可能性にさらなる責任を担う
item.description: 最先端のサーバーチップは、そのパフォーマンス向上の可能性で注目を集めていますが、次世代プロセッサは旧来の設計よりも動作温度が高くなるため、データセンター事業者は、チップメーカーからの限られたガイダンスの中で何をすべきかを見極めるのに苦労するでしょう。 それと同時に、エネルギー効率化の取り組みにおいてIT機器が果たす役割に対しても、厳しい目が向けられることになります。これらの相互に関連するトレンドは、Uptime Instituteが今年のデータセンターについて予測する上位に挙げているものの一つです。 「事業者は、高温になる新しいサーバーチップに苦戦することになるでしょう」とUptimeのリサー
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: AI can predict extreme weather, but here’s where the forecasts fall short
item.description: When floods ripped through parts of Europe in September, the scale of the destruction took people by surprise. The intense rains should not have, because those had been predicted by sophisticated forecasting systems enhanced with artificial intelligence. But forewarned did not mean forearmed.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Intel, AMD unite in new x86 alliance to tackle AI, other challenges
item.description: Semiconductor rivals Intel and AMD announced the formation of an x86-processor advisory group that will try to address ever-increasing AI workloads, custom chiplets, and advances in 3D packaging and system architectures.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Netskope extends data security with Dasera acquisition
item.description: Netskope today announced it has broadened its data protection capabilities by integrating data security posture management (DSPM) technology into its Netskope One Platform via its acquisition of Dasera.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump says he likes Mark Zuckerberg 'much better now'
item.description: Former President Trump said he likes Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg “much better now,” as the head of Facebook and Instagram increasingly looks to stay out of politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google Endorses Bill Requiring Disclosure of Litigation Funding - A Step Towards Transparency in the Justice System?
item.description: Google supports a bill that would require plaintiffs to disclose investors funding lawsuits, aiming to reduce excessive litigation and promote transparency in the justice system.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Google bets on nuclear power to drive AI expansion
item.description: Google has signed its first corporate deal to purchase power from multiple small modular reactors (SMRs) to meet the energy needs of its AI systems, marking a key step as AI companies shift toward nuclear power.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why are we still confused about cloud security?
item.description: A report by cloud security company Tenable discovered that 74% of companies surveyed had exposed storage or other misconfigurations. This is a dangerous open door to cybercriminals. Overall, cloud security is getting worse.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple accused of restricting workers' Slack, social media use
item.description: The National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) filed a complaint against Apple on Thursday, accusing the iPhone maker of improperly restricting workers’ use of Slack and other social media.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The secret to reading (yes, reading!) podcasts in your inbox
item.description: When we talk about email tools, we typically talk about apps or services that help you tame the wild jungle of your ever-overflowing inbox. Today’s tool is a twist. It’s about email, through and through—but it’s actually a smart new service that adds something to that environment.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Will your AI bot put citizens at risk?
item.description: Government agencies have an obligation to provide accurate information to citizens, and a bad bot can have both legal and moral implications. The post Will your AI bot put citizens at risk? first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Gemini Code Assist Enterprise woos enterprise developers
item.description: Google Cloud has announced Gemini Code Assist Enterprise, billed as an enterprise-grade tool that lets developers generate or transform code that is more accurate and relevant to their applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World
